title: 10 best herbal teas for lice
intro_desc: Herbal teas for lice are natural remedies made from various plants and herbs, which have been used for centuries as a safe and effective alternative to chemical-based treatments for head lice. These teas work by creating an unfavorable environment for lice and nits, making it difficult for them to survive or reproduce. Examples of herbal teas that help with lice include tea tree oil, lavender, eucalyptus, and rosemary. By using these herbal teas, individuals can avoid the potential side effects of chemical treatments while effectively eliminating lice and nits. This not only improves the health and well-being of those affected but also reduces the risk of re-infestation. Additionally, herbal teas for lice are often more affordable and accessible than chemical treatments, making them a practical and convenient solution for many families.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Neem tea helps with lice because it contains natural insecticidal properties that can effectively kill and repel head lice. The active compounds in neem, such as azadirachtin, disrupt the life cycle of lice and inhibit their ability to feed, eventually leading to their demise. Moreover, neem tea has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can alleviate itching and prevent secondary infections caused by scratching. Regular consumption of neem tea or topical application of neem-infused solutions can provide a chemical-free and safe alternative to combat head lice infestations.
remedy_properties: Insecticidal properties: Neem tea contains azadirachtin, a compound that disrupts the life cycle of lice, preventing them from reproducing and feeding.
Anthelmintic properties: Neem tea has the ability to paralyze and kill lice, making it an effective natural treatment for lice infestations.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Neem tea can help reduce itching and inflammation caused by lice bites, providing relief for those affected.
Antimicrobial properties: Neem tea has antiseptic and antimicrobial properties that can help prevent secondary infections caused by scratching.
Immune-boosting properties: Neem tea can help strengthen the immune system, making it easier for the body to fight off lice and other parasites.
remedy_recipe: Boil 4 cups of water in a pot, then add 2 tablespoons of dried neem leaves.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 15-20 minutes.
Strain the tea into a container, discarding the leaves.
Allow the tea to cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Pour the neem tea over the scalp and hair, massaging it gently, then leave it on for 30 minutes before rinsing.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Narrow-leaved paperbark tea helps with lice because it contains natural compounds that can inhibit the growth and reproduction of lice. The tea is made from the leaves of the narrow-leaved paperbark tree, which has been used in traditional medicine for centuries to treat a variety of ailments. The leaves contain essential oils, such as eucalyptus oil, that have antimicrobial and insecticidal properties. These properties make the tea an effective natural remedy for getting rid of lice. The tea can be used as a rinse for the hair and scalp, and it can help to alleviate itching and irritation caused by lice. Additionally, the tea can be used to make a spray that can be applied to bedding and furniture to kill any lice that may have migrated from the scalp.
remedy_properties: Antiparasitic: Narrow-leaved paperbark tea contains compounds that kill lice and their eggs, making it effective in eliminating lice infestations. The active constituents responsible for this property include terpene alcohols and sesquiterpene hydrocarbons.

Anti-inflammatory: The tea helps reduce skin inflammation and irritation caused by lice bites, providing relief and promoting healing. This is due to the presence of various flavonoids and phenolic acids in the bark.

Antimicrobial: Narrow-leaved paperbark tea inhibits the growth of bacteria and fungi, preventing secondary infections that may occur from scratching lice bites. The antimicrobial properties are attributed to the high concentration of tannins and essential oils in the bark.

Analgesic: The tea helps alleviate itching and pain associated with lice infestations by numbing the affected area. This is achieved through the presence of various volatile oils and alkaloids in the bark.

Immune-boosting: Drinking the tea can help strengthen the immune system, making it more effective at fighting off infections and parasites like lice. This is due to the high concentration of antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals found in the bark.
remedy_recipe: Collect 2-3 narrow-leaved paperbark (Melaleuca squamea) leaves, ensuring they are free from pesticides and other contaminants.
Roughly crush the leaves to release their oils, allowing them to steep more effectively in hot water.
Place the crushed leaves in a pot and add 1 liter of water, bringing it to a boil.
Simmer the mixture for 15-20 minutes to extract the beneficial compounds from the leaves.
Strain the tea, discarding the leaves, and let it cool down to a drinkable temperature before using it as a lice treatment.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary tea helps with lice because it has natural properties that can effectively kill and repel head lice. The essential oils found in rosemary, such as eucalyptol and alpha-pinene, have insecticidal and repellent effects that can help get rid of lice and prevent future infestations. Moreover, rosemary tea is a natural and safe alternative to chemical-based treatments, making it a popular choice for those looking for a more gentle and holistic approach to treating head lice. Regular use of rosemary tea as a rinse or hair treatment can help keep lice at bay and promote healthy hair and scalp.
remedy_properties: Anti-parasitic property: Rosemary tea has the ability to kill lice and their eggs due to its active constituents such as camphor, eucalyptol, and alpha-pinene.
Insect-repellent property: Rosemary tea acts as a natural repellent for lice, preventing infestations and keeping them at bay.
Anti-inflammatory property: Rosemary tea helps soothe itchy and inflamed scalps caused by lice, reducing discomfort and promoting healing.
Antimicrobial property: Rosemary tea has the ability to prevent and treat secondary bacterial infections that may occur due to lice infestations.
Hair growth promotion property: Regular use of rosemary tea can promote hair growth and strengthen hair follicles, which can help restore the damage caused by lice infestations.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 2 cups of water and add 2-3 fresh rosemary sprigs (or 1-2 teaspoons of dried rosemary leaves).
Reduce heat, cover, and let it simmer for 15-20 minutes to extract the herb's properties.
Strain the liquid to remove rosemary leaves and let it cool down to a warm temperature.
Add honey or lemon juice (optional) for taste, stir well, and pour into a cup.
Drink the tea once or twice a day, preferably before meals, for a few days to help combat lice infestation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Oregano tea helps with lice because it contains powerful compounds such as carvacrol and thymol, which have antiparasitic and insecticidal properties. These compounds can help to kill lice and prevent them from reproducing. Additionally, oregano tea has anti-inflammatory and soothing effects, which can help to alleviate itching and irritation caused by lice. Drinking oregano tea or using it as a rinse can help to eliminate lice and promote healthy hair and scalp. However, it is important to note that oregano tea should not be used as a sole treatment for lice and should be used in conjunction with other proven methods such as combing and washing.
remedy_properties: Antiparasitic: Oregano tea contains compounds such as carvacrol and thymol, which have been shown to kill lice and eggs, making it an effective natural treatment for head lice.
Anti-inflammatory: Oregano tea can help reduce inflammation and itching caused by lice bites, providing relief to those affected.
Antimicrobial: Oregano tea has antimicrobial properties that can help prevent skin infections caused by scratching and excessive picking at the scalp due to lice infestation.
Antioxidant: Oregano tea is rich in antioxidants, which can help boost the immune system and aid in the body's natural defenses against lice and other parasites.
Soothing: Oregano tea has a calming effect on the skin and can help soothe and relax the scalp, making it easier to remove lice and eggs.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 2 cups of water and add 2 tablespoons of dried oregano leaves, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain the oregano tea into a container, discarding the used oregano leaves.
Allow the tea to cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Wet the hair and scalp, then evenly pour the oregano tea over the head, massaging it into the scalp.
Cover the head with a shower cap and let it sit for 30 minutes before rinsing it out with water.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme tea helps with lice because it contains natural properties that can effectively kill and repel lice. Thymol, a compound found in thyme, has insecticidal properties that can eliminate lice and prevent them from multiplying. Moreover, thyme tea has a strong aroma that can help repel lice and keep them away from the hair and scalp. Regularly consuming thyme tea or using it as a rinse can help reduce the risk of lice infestations and promote healthy hair growth. Furthermore, thyme tea is a natural and safe alternative to chemical-based lice treatments, making it an ideal solution for people who prefer to avoid harsh chemicals.
remedy_properties: Antiparasitic: Thyme tea has antiparasitic properties that can help kill lice and their eggs, making it an effective natural treatment for head lice. This is due to the presence of active compounds such as thymol and carvacrol.

Anti-inflammatory: Thyme tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe the scalp and reduce irritation caused by lice bites. This is attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids.

Antimicrobial: Thyme tea has antimicrobial properties that can help prevent secondary infections caused by scratching the scalp due to lice bites. This is due to the presence of volatile oils such as thymol and carvacrol.

Analgesic: Thyme tea has analgesic properties that can help relieve pain and itching caused by lice infestations. This is attributed to the presence of compounds such as thymol, carvacrol, and flavonoids.
Calming: Thyme tea has calming properties that can help reduce stress and anxiety caused by lice infestations. This is due to the presence of volatile oils such as linalool and camphene.
remedy_recipe: Boil 2 cups of water and add 2-3 tablespoons of dried thyme leaves, then let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the liquid to remove the thyme leaves, leaving you with thyme tea.
Allow the tea to cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Rinse or spray the thyme tea onto the scalp and hair, ensuring complete coverage.
Leave the thyme tea on the hair for 30 minutes before rinsing it out thoroughly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum tea helps with lice because it contains powerful compounds that can effectively kill and repel head lice. The tea is made from the leaves of the Tasmanian blue gum tree, which is native to Australia and has been used for centuries by indigenous people for its medicinal properties. The tea is rich in terpenoids, particularly 1,8-cineole, which has been shown to have insecticidal and repellent effects against head lice. By drinking the tea or using it as a rinse, the compounds in the tea can help to eliminate existing lice and prevent future infestations. Additionally, the tea has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe and heal any irritation or infection caused by the lice.
remedy_properties: Insecticidal properties: The oil in Tasmanian blue gum tea contains terpinen-4-ol, which has been shown to have insecticidal effects on lice, helping to kill them and reduce infestations.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The tea contains compounds such as alpha-pinene and limonene, which have anti-inflammatory effects, helping to soothe and heal the scalp from the itching and irritation caused by lice.
Antimicrobial properties: The oil in Tasmanian blue gum tea has antimicrobial properties, which can help prevent secondary infections on the scalp caused by scratching and bites from lice.
Analgesic properties: The tea contains compounds such as eucalyptol and alpha-pinene, which have analgesic effects, helping to relieve the pain and discomfort caused by lice.
Antipruritic properties: The oil in Tasmanian blue gum tea has antipruritic properties, which can help reduce itching and scratching, providing relief from the symptoms of lice infestations.
remedy_recipe: Boil 2 cups of water and add 2 tablespoons of dried Tasmanian blue gum leaves; let it simmer for 15 minutes.
Turn off the heat and allow the mixture to steep for an additional 10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the leaves, reserving the liquid.
Allow the tea to cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Use the tea as a final rinse after shampooing and while the hair is still damp, optionally covering the head with a shower cap for better absorption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage tea helps with lice because it contains natural compounds that can inhibit the growth and reproduction of lice. The active constituents in sage, such as camphor and camphene, have insecticidal properties that can help to eliminate lice and prevent further infestations. Moreover, sage tea has a strong aroma that can help to repel lice and keep them away from the hair and scalp. Regular use of sage tea as a rinse or hair treatment can help to maintain a lice-free environment and promote healthy hair and scalp.
remedy_properties: Antiparasitic: Sage tea has antiparasitic properties that can help to kill lice and their eggs, with active constituents such as camphor and camphene.
Anti-inflammatory: Sage tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe and heal the scalp from lice bites and irritation.
Antimicrobial: Sage tea has antimicrobial properties that can help to prevent and treat any secondary infections that may occur as a result of lice infestation.
Astringent: Sage tea has astringent properties that can help to cleanse and tighten the pores on the scalp, making it less hospitable to lice.
Vermifuge: Sage tea has vermifuge properties that can help to expel intestinal worms and other parasites, which can help to prevent re-infestation of lice.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, bring 4 cups of water to a boil.
Add 1 cup of fresh sage leaves or 1/2 cup of dried sage leaves to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let the mixture simmer for 15-20 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the sage leaves.
Allow the tea to cool down to a comfortable temperature before using it as a rinse on the hair and scalp.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood tea helps with lice because it contains natural compounds that can effectively kill and repel lice. The active ingredient in wormwood, called artemisinin, has been shown to have insecticidal properties that can help eliminate lice infestations. Additionally, wormwood tea has a strong smell that can repel lice and prevent them from infesting the hair and scalp. Drinking wormwood tea can also help boost the immune system, making it easier for the body to fight off lice infestations and prevent future outbreaks. Overall, wormwood tea is a natural and effective solution for treating and preventing lice infestations.
remedy_properties: Insecticidal properties: Wormwood tea contains compounds such as artemisinin and thujone, which have insecticidal properties that can help to kill lice and their eggs, making it an effective natural treatment for head lice.

Antiparasitic properties: The antiparasitic properties of wormwood tea, due to the presence of artemisinin and other compounds, can help to eliminate lice infestations by inhibiting the growth and development of lice.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Wormwood tea has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe the scalp and reduce inflammation caused by scratching and biting from lice.
Analgesic properties: The analgesic properties of wormwood tea can help to alleviate the itching and discomfort associated with lice infestations, providing relief for those affected.

Antimicrobial properties: Wormwood tea has antimicrobial properties, which can help to prevent secondary infections that may occur as a result of lice bites, keeping the affected area clean and healthy.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 2 cups of water and 2 tablespoons of dried wormwood herb, then bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce heat, cover the pot, and let it simmer for 15-20 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the herb particles, then let it cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Use the wormwood tea as a final rinse after washing and conditioning the hair.
Leave the tea on the hair for 10-15 minutes before rinsing it out with water.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tansy tea helps with lice because it contains compounds such as thujone and artemisinin, which have insecticidal properties. These compounds can help to kill lice and their eggs, making it an effective natural treatment for head lice. Tansy tea can be applied directly to the scalp and left on for a period of time before being washed out. This can help to eliminate lice and prevent them from returning. It is important to note that while tansy tea can be effective in treating lice, it should not be used as a substitute for medical treatment, especially in severe cases of lice infestation.
remedy_properties: Insecticidal properties: Tansy tea contains compounds such as thujone and isothujone, which have insecticidal effects that can help to kill lice and prevent their eggs from hatching.
Antipruritic properties: Tansy tea has anti-inflammatory and soothing effects that can help to alleviate the itching and irritation caused by lice infestations.
Analgesic properties: Tansy tea contains compounds such as camphor and borneol, which have pain-relieving effects that can help to reduce the discomfort associated with lice bites.
Antimicrobial properties: Tansy tea has antimicrobial properties that can help to prevent secondary infections caused by scratching and biting of the skin.

Sedative properties: Tansy tea contains compounds such as artemisinin and flavonoids, which have sedative effects that can help to promote relaxation and reduce stress associated with lice infestations.
remedy_recipe: Boil one quart of water in a pot.
Add two teaspoons of dried tansy herb to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let the mixture simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the herbs, then let it cool.
Drink one cup of the tansy tea daily as a lice preventative.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Horsetail tea helps with lice because it contains natural compounds that can inhibit the growth and reproduction of these parasites. Horsetail is rich in silica, a mineral that can help to strengthen hair and make it less susceptible to lice infestations. Additionally, horsetail tea has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe itchy scalp and prevent secondary infections that can occur as a result of scratching. Drinking horsetail tea can also help to boost the immune system, making it easier for the body to fight off lice infestations and other infections. However, it is important to note that horsetail tea is not a guaranteed cure for lice and should be used as part of a comprehensive treatment plan that includes other lice-removal methods.
remedy_properties: Anti-parasitic: Horsetail tea contains compounds such as silica and flavonoids that can help to kill and inhibit the growth of lice.
Astringent: The astringent properties of horsetail tea, due to its tannin content, can help to eliminate lice by drying out their exoskeleton and causing them to dehydrate.
Antimicrobial: Horsetail tea has antimicrobial properties that can help to prevent and treat secondary infections that can occur as a result of lice infestations.
Anti-inflammatory: The anti-inflammatory properties of horsetail tea can help to reduce itching and irritation caused by lice bites.
Diuretic: Horsetail tea has diuretic properties, which can help to flush out toxins and impurities from the body, including those caused by lice.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 2 tablespoons of dried horsetail herb.
Boil 4 cups of water in a pot for 5 minutes.
Add the horsetail herb to the boiling water, then reduce heat and simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the herb, then let it cool to a comfortable temperature.
Use the horsetail tea as a hair rinse after shampooing, leaving it on for 10-15 minutes before rinsing out.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat lice with herbal teas is by using infusions made from potent herbs such as tea tree, lavender, and peppermint. These herbs have natural properties that can help eradicate lice and nits. Brew a strong tea, let it cool, and then carefully apply it to the scalp and hair. Massage it in, cover the head with a shower cap, and leave it on for about 30 minutes before rinsing thoroughly. Repeat this process every other day for a week to ensure complete elimination of lice and nits. Remember to wash all bedding and clothing in hot water to prevent re-infestation.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to lice that are treated with herbal teas include scalp infections and dandruff. Certain herbal teas like chamomile, calendula, and nettle have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help soothe an itchy scalp, reduce inflammation, and combat fungal or bacterial infections. These herbal teas can be used as a rinse after shampooing or steeped and applied directly to the scalp. However, it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before starting any new treatment to ensure safety and effectiveness.
